---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Accepted publication, thesis work, and the questions I want to keep answering"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="research-shell">
  <section class="research-hero">
    <span class="section-eyebrow">Research</span>
    <h1 class="research-title">Studying how financing, governance, and delivery systems shape chronic care in rural Kenya.</h1>
    <p class="research-lead">My first-author mixed-methods manuscript on hypertension and diabetes care in Seme Sub-County has been accepted for publication in <em>BMC Public Health</em>. I am currently finalizing the thesis and administrative requirements for expected graduation in December 2026.</p>
    <div class="research-actions">
      <a href="thesis.html" class="btn-solid">View thesis</a>
      <a href="publications_files/01_NCDFin_ManuscriptV4.pdf" class="btn-ghost" target="_blank">Read manuscript PDF</a>
      <a href="../posts/47-facility-decision-space-chronic-care/index.html" class="btn-ghost">Read plain-language essay</a>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="research-kpi-grid" aria-label="Research milestones">
    <article class="research-kpi-card">
      <span class="research-kpi-value">BMC</span>
      <span class="research-kpi-label">First-author manuscript accepted in BMC Public Health</span>
    </article>
    <article class="research-kpi-card">
      <span class="research-kpi-value">7</span>
      <span class="research-kpi-label">Public primary-care facilities studied in Seme Sub-County</span>
    </article>
    <article class="research-kpi-card">
      <span class="research-kpi-value">Dec 2026</span>
      <span class="research-kpi-label">Expected MSc graduation after thesis clearance</span>
    </article>
  </section>
</div>
```

## Featured publication

```{=html}
<article class="research-pub-card">
  <p class="research-pub-meta">Accepted manuscript · BMC Public Health</p>
  <h3>Financial Determinants of Effective Hypertension and Diabetes Care in Rural Primary Health Facilities in Kisumu, Kenya: A Mixed-Methods Study</h3>
  <p class="research-pub-lead">This first-author study examines how Kenya's devolved financing architecture shapes chronic care delivery for hypertension and diabetes in seven rural public facilities. The central finding is blunt: facilities can plan for NCD care, but without spending autonomy and protected budgets they cannot reliably sustain it.</p>
  <div class="research-stat-grid">
    <div class="research-stat">
      <strong>71.4%</strong>
      <span>depended mainly on NHIF reimbursements and donor support</span>
    </div>
    <div class="research-stat">
      <strong>0%</strong>
      <span>had full spending autonomy despite holding bank accounts</span>
    </div>
    <div class="research-stat">
      <strong>85.7%</strong>
      <span>reported frequent medication stockouts</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</article>
```

- Core contribution: shows that chronic-care failure in rural primary care is often a governance and decision-space problem, not only a funding-volume problem.
- Methods: convergent parallel mixed methods using structured facility questionnaires, retrospective financial record review, and interviews with facility in-charges.
- Policy value: provides a baseline for evaluating the transition from NHIF to the Social Health Authority and for redesigning facility-level financial autonomy.
- Public writing version: [Why facility decision space matters for chronic care in rural Kenya](../posts/47-facility-decision-space-chronic-care/index.qmd)

## Selected contributions

1. **Amollo, N. W.**, Ogol, J., Museve, E., Owenga, J. A., Aduda, D. O., Onguru, D. *Financial Determinants of Effective Hypertension and Diabetes Care in Rural Primary Health Facilities in Kisumu, Kenya: A Mixed-Methods Study.* Accepted in *BMC Public Health*.
   Role: First author; study design, mixed-methods analysis, interpretation, and writing.

2. Ouma, O. J., Omondi, D., Akinyi, I., **Amollo, N. W.**, Ogutu, S., Obinge, E., et al. *Addressing priority gaps in access and quality of NCD services in primary care settings in rural Kenya: A participatory approach to intervention development.*
   Role: Conceptualization, data collection, management, analysis, and co-writing results and discussion.

3. Mangale, D. I., Adhiambo, H., Adagi, P. A., Nyandieka, E., Abente, B., Achieng, S., **Amollo, N. W.**, and Odeny, T. A. *Characteristics and mortality risk among esophageal cancer patients with varied HIV status seeking care in western Kenya: A 10-year analysis.*
   Role: Data management and analysis.

4. Odeny, T. A., Adhiambo, H. F., Mangale, D. I., Were, N. A., Nyandieka, E., Adagi, P. A., **Amollo, N. W.**, and Atieno, D. *Survival disparities in cervical cancer patients with and without HIV at Jaramogi Oginga Odinga Teaching and Referral Hospital.*
   Role: Data management and analysis.

## Research interests

```{=html}
<div class="research-interest-grid">
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>Health financing and decision space</h4>
    <p>How county financing rules, reimbursement flows, and facility autonomy shape frontline service delivery.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>NCD care in primary health systems</h4>
    <p>Hypertension and diabetes management, medicine continuity, and the operational realities of rural chronic care.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>Mixed-methods health systems research</h4>
    <p>Combining routine records, structured facility data, interviews, and implementation context into policy-relevant evidence.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>Life-course diabetes epidemiology</h4>
    <p>Urbanization, work history, and the social pathways through which diabetes risk accumulates across the life course.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>Community-delivered intervention design</h4>
    <p>Participatory models for lifestyle support, diabetes self-management, and locally grounded implementation strategies.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-interest-card">
    <h4>Research data systems and reproducibility</h4>
    <p>Practical data architecture, survey workflows, and transparent reporting for health research teams.</p>
  </article>
</div>
```

## Current agenda

```{=html}
<div class="research-track-grid">
  <article class="research-track-card">
    <p class="research-track-kicker">Now</p>
    <h4>Thesis completion</h4>
    <p>Finalizing thesis revisions, presentation materials, and administrative requirements for expected graduation in December 2026.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-track-card">
    <p class="research-track-kicker">Next</p>
    <h4>SHA transition follow-up</h4>
    <p>Building on the accepted paper to study how the Social Health Authority changes facility liquidity, approvals, and chronic-care continuity.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="research-track-card">
    <p class="research-track-kicker">Developing</p>
    <h4>Diabetes risk and community pathways</h4>
    <p>Extending the financing work toward life-course diabetes risk, community-based lifestyle modification, and implementation science in rural Kenya.</p>
  </article>
</div>
```

## Collaboration

I am interested in collaborations on health systems research, chronic disease management, mixed-methods implementation studies, research data systems, and public-health analytics in Africa.

[Email me](mailto:nichodemuswerre@gmail.com){.btn-solid} [View the thesis page](thesis.qmd){.btn-ghost}
